diagrams.title: Single-Level vs Multi-Level Indexing Structure
diagrams.description: Shows comparison between single-level indexing (one index file) and multi-level indexing (hierarchical index levels)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how multi-level indexing reduces disk I/O by creating hierarchical search paths

diagrams.title: B+ Tree Index Structure
diagrams.description: Complete B+ tree showing internal nodes with keys and pointers, leaf nodes with data and horizontal links
elements: Leaf nodes with actual data pointers
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how B+ tree organizes index entries and supports efficient range queries

diagrams.title: Dense vs Sparse Index Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing dense index (entry per record) and sparse index (entry per block)
elements: Data file with 12 records in 3 blocks
Dense index: 12 index entries (one per record)
Sparse index: 3 index entries (one per block)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding storage vs speed trade-off between dense and sparse indexing
